# My Train

> A zero-typing Progressive Web App that uses the visitor's geolocation to tell a
> UK rail passenger what train they're on and every remaining stop, with live
> ETAs, platforms and delay status. Built for one-handed use on a moving train
> with poor signal: it opens full-screen, fires GPS automatically, and never
> shows a keyboard.

Live site: https://what-train.ocoomber.workers.dev/

## How it works

The app has two main screens:

- **Pick your train** — shows the nearest station's live departures as big
  tappable cards. If the visitor is moving fast with a clear bearing, it leads
  with a best-guess "Are you on this train?" card. No text input anywhere.
- **Track your train** — once a service is chosen it locks on and shows where the
  train is now plus every remaining stop to the destination, auto-refreshing
  every 60 seconds, until the visitor taps "Different train".

A locked train has a shareable deep link: `?train=<serviceId>` points straight at
that exact service, so it can be bookmarked, shared, or turned into a QR code.

## For agents

- Interactive controls carry a stable `data-agent-target` attribute keyed to
  function (e.g. `select-train`, `refresh-train`, `share-train`,
  `pick-different-train`, `nearby-stations`, `open-help`). Prefer these over
  CSS/position selectors — they survive restyles.
- Loading state is exposed as `aria-busy` on the `#screen` element; the status
  header (`#status-text`) is an `aria-live` region announcing state transitions.

## API

The site and its API share one origin (a Cloudflare Worker). The API proxies the
Realtime Trains API v2 with the bearer token kept server-side:

- `GET /api/health` — liveness check, returns `{"ok":true}`.
- `GET /api/board/:crs` — live departure board for a 3-letter CRS station code.
- `GET /api/service?uid=<uniqueIdentity>` — full calling-point detail for one
  service.

## Credits

Station coordinates derived from the public UK-Train-Station-Locations dataset.
Train data from Realtime Trains (https://www.realtimetrains.co.uk/).
